---
title: "visualization 1 :)"
format:
  html:
    self-contained: true
---
```{r}
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false

library(tidyverse)
library(readxl)
library(gapminder)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(here)
library(janitor)
library(gtsummary)
library(broom)
library(gt)
library(ghibli)

data <- read_excel(here("data/gapminder_broadband_per_100.xlsx")) %>% 
  janitor::clean_names()
data <- data %>% replace(is.na(.), 0) %>% 
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), round, 5))
gapminder_second <- gapminder %>% 
  group_by(continent) %>% 
  summarise(min_lifeexp = min(lifeExp),
            max_lifeexp = max(lifeExp))

ggplot(gapminder_second, 
       aes(y = continent)) +
  geom_segment(aes(x = min_lifeexp, 
                   xend = max_lifeexp, 
                   yend = continent),
               color = "#b8c9bf", linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_point(aes(x = min_lifeexp, color = "Minimum"), size = 1, shape = 8) +
  geom_point(aes(x = max_lifeexp, color = "Maximum"), size = 1, shape = 8) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c("Minimum" = "#6b9e8f", "Maximum" = "#c4956a")
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Life expectancy average by continents",
    x = "Average life expectancy in years",
    y = "Continents",
    color = "Life expectancy"
  )
```

The visualization above is from Lab 4 where we explored graph building! I did the average life expectancy across each country across the different years, and then I did the average life expectancy in each country. The graph itself is the average minimum versus average maximum years in terms of life expectancy grouped by continents.
